skill.name: deployment-procedures
skill.description: Production deployment principles and decision-making. Safe deployment workflows, rollback strategies, and verification. Teaches thinking, not scripts.
skill.summary: Learn deployment decision-making for safe production releases with rollback strategies and verification workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The backtick patterns at SKILL.md:30,45,87 are markdown code block delimiters for documentation diagrams, not shell execution. The cryptographic algorithm warning at SKILL.md:3,246 has no basis - these lines contain YAML frontmatter and plain text. This is educational documentation with no executable code, network calls, or filesystem operations.

content.user_title: Deploy production releases with confidence and safety
content.value_statement: Production deployments are risky without proper methodology. This skill teaches decision-making frameworks for safe deployments, rollback strategies, and verification workflows that minimize downtime.
actual_capabilities: Guide platform selection for different application types (static sites, web apps, microservices, serverless)
Provide pre-deployment verification checklists covering code quality, build, environment, and safety
Execute structured 5-phase deployment workflow (Prepare, Backup, Deploy, Verify, Confirm)
Recommend rollback strategies based on platform and risk level
Support zero-downtime deployment approaches (rolling, blue-green, canary)
Assist with emergency procedures and post-deployment verification
limitations: Does not execute deployment commands automatically - provides guidance only
Platform-specific procedures require manual adaptation to your infrastructure
Cannot access your deployment environment, logs, or monitoring systems directly
Does not replace formal change management or approval processes required by your organization

use_cases.title: Development Team Lead
use_cases.description: Establish consistent deployment procedures across multiple projects and team members with standardized checklists and verification steps.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering managers and team leads responsible for deployment reliability

use_cases.title: Solo Developer
use_cases.description: Learn production deployment best practices without enterprise tooling, using platform-appropriate workflows for personal or small business projects.
use_cases.target_user: Independent developers managing their own deployments

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer
use_cases.description: Document and communicate deployment runbooks, train junior engineers on deployment decision-making, and establish rollback procedures.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps professionals responsible for release management

prompt_templates.title: Pre-deployment checklist review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my deployment plan for a Node.js API on Railway. I have tests passing, code reviewed, and production build ready. Walk me through the remaining pre-deployment checks and help me prepare a rollback plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: First-time deployment with managed platform

prompt_templates.title: Platform selection guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a React frontend with a Python FastAPI backend. Help me choose the right deployment platform considering: low cost, easy rollback, and minimal DevOps overhead. Compare at least 3 options with their deployment procedures.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Evaluating deployment options for new project

prompt_templates.title: Zero-downtime deployment planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a zero-downtime deployment for our e-commerce site on Kubernetes. We need to deploy a database migration that adds a non-nullable column. Outline the strategy, verification steps, and rollback triggers.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Managing complex deployments with database changes

prompt_templates.title: Emergency rollback execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Our production deployment just failed - the service is returning 502 errors. We deployed to VPS using PM2. Guide me through the immediate steps to assess the situation and execute a rollback if needed.

output_examples.input: Pre-deployment checklist for static site on Vercel
output: 1. Code Quality: Tests passing, linting clean, PR approved
2. Build: Run local build, verify no errors or warnings
3. Environment: Confirm Vercel env vars match requirements
4. Safety: Note current deployment commit for rollback reference
5. Notification: Alert team of deployment window
Deployment: Push to main branch triggers auto-deploy
Verification: Check deployment logs, test live URL, verify critical flows

output_examples.input: Rollback strategy for failed Rails deployment on Render
output: Immediate Action: Navigate to Render dashboard > Service > Deployments
Select previous successful deployment from list
Click 'Deploy' to rollback to that commit
Monitor logs for successful startup (typically 2-5 minutes)
Test critical user flows (login, core features)
Notify team: Rollback completed, incident investigation scheduled
Post-incident: Schedule post-mortem after service stability confirmed

best_practices: Deploy small changes frequently rather than large releases with multiple modifications
Always have a tested rollback plan before initiating any production deployment
Monitor actively for at least 15 minutes after deployment before considering it complete
anti_patterns: Deploying on Friday or before holidays without on-call coverage ready
Skipping staging environment testing to save time on urgent releases
Walking away immediately after deployment command completes without verification

faq.question: Does this skill execute deployment commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance, checklists, and decision-making frameworks. You execute the actual deployment commands. This approach ensures you understand each step and maintain control over your production environment.

faq.question: Which deployment platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers principles applicable to all platforms: Vercel, Netlify, Railway, Render, Fly.io, VPS with PM2, Docker, and Kubernetes. Specific procedures vary by platform, but the decision-making framework applies universally.

faq.question: How do I know if I should rollback or fix forward?
faq.answer: Rollback immediately if: service is down, critical errors occur, or performance is degraded more than 50 percent. Fix forward only for minor issues that can be resolved quickly. When in doubt, rollback first and debug later.

faq.question: What is the minimum monitoring window after deployment?
faq.answer: Actively monitor for 15 minutes minimum. Check health endpoints and error logs in the first 5 minutes. Test key user flows within 15 minutes. Review performance metrics the next day for final confirmation.

faq.question: Can this skill help with database migrations during deployment?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides guidance on deployment sequencing for database changes, including strategies for backward-compatible migrations and handling migrations that require downtime. Always test migrations in staging first.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for teams without dedicated DevOps engineers?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed for developers who need to handle their own deployments. It emphasizes understanding principles over memorizing commands, making it accessible for teams learning deployment best practices.
